exemplar.title: MYP personal Project - designing a dress based on the layers of the skin

detailedExemplarData.title: MYP personal Project - designing a dress based on the layers of the skin

strengths: Synthesis of skin anatomy research into fabric selection, showing strong application of conceptual knowledge to design
Demonstrated self-management skills in organizing extensive out-of-class work, illustrating effective ATL use
Robust research skills evidenced by evaluating multiple medical sources and consulting experts (chemistry teacher, designers)
Detailed examples of sewing techniques learned and challenges overcome, enhancing product quality
weaknesses: Brief critique of the personal blogâs reliability; lacks identification of specific unsupported claims or citation gaps
Incomplete evaluation of the Stanford Medicine source, missing a full justification against reliability criteria
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student effectively applied ATL skills across research, self-management and technical skill acquisition, enriching the dress design and ensuring quality. To deepen this impact, critiques of sources should include specific examples of unsupported claims or missing citations, and each evaluated source needs a complete justification against established reliability criteria.

strengths: Clear connection of personal interests and strengths to the learning goal, which demonstrates thoughtful planning and sustained motivation
Comprehensive action plan with detailed to-dos, dates and sequencing, including buffer weeks for realistic time management
Effective resource and financial planning, evidenced by adherence to a clear â¬450 budget
weaknesses: Incomplete AccessFM table entries (Material, Customer), lacking fully defined, measurable success indicators
Broad learning goal (âlearn about the chemistry of the skin and fashionâ) without specific, measurable targets
Several action-plan tasks use placeholders (ââ¦â) instead of concrete steps and deadlines
Customer criterion outlines actions but lacks observable metrics (e.g., survey results) to assess comfort and accessibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning section is a clear strength, with a well-sequenced timeline, realistic buffers and sound budget management. The strong link to personal interest will drive engagement. To reach the highest level, the student should refine the learning goal into specific, measurable outcomes, complete all AccessFM criteria with clear success indicators and replace placeholders with detailed actions and deadlines.

strengths: Thoughtful reflection on material technique adjustments (switching to fabric glue), demonstrating adaptability and problem-solving
Clear articulation of personal growth in time management, problem-solving and sewing skills, linking experiences to learning
weaknesses: Product evaluation is too general; does not assess each success criterion explicitly with supporting evidence
Feedback mechanisms (teacher form) rely solely on quantitative data, missing qualitative insights into reasoning
Reflection on future improvements (e.g., creating varied colorways) lacks a concrete implementation plan
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Reflections reveal critical thinking and clear recognition of skills gained, but to fully meet the criterion the student should structure evaluations against each success criterion with specific evidence, incorporate qualitative feedback for richer insight and outline detailed steps for implementing future project suggestions.

annotations.comment: You clearly connect personal interests and strengths, which demonstrates thoughtful planning and will sustain your motivation throughout the project.
The AccessFM table is a strong framework, but several rows (Material, Customer) are incomplete. Ensure each criterion is fully defined with measurable success indicators.
Your critique of the personal blogâs reliability is brief. Expand it by identifying specific unsupported claims or the absence of verifiable citations.
Your synthesis of skin anatomy knowledge into fabric choices shows clear application of research to design, reinforcing the conceptual coherence of your dress.

Your learning goal âlearn about the chemistry of the skin and fashionâ is broad. Rephrase it with specific, measurable targets (for example, define three key skin chemistry concepts) to clarify how you will know when itâs achieved.
The Customer criterion outlines actions but lacks clear metrics for comfort and accessibility. Define observable indicators (e.g., peer survey results) to assess success.
Your comprehensive action plan with dates, detailed to-dos and linked success criteria reflects thorough sequencing and resource planning, which will guide your process effectively.
The detailed timeline, including buffer weeks for unexpected delays, demonstrates excellent planning and realistic time management.
Several tasks in your action plan use placeholders (ââ¦â) instead of specific steps. Replace these with concrete actions and deadlines to maintain clarity and accountability.
The evaluation of the Stanford Medicine source is incomplete. Provide a full justification of its reliability criteria rather than trailing off, to strengthen your analysis.
You set and adhered to a clear â¬450 budget, showcasing effective financial planning and resource management.
Your explanation of how self-management guided extensive out-of-class work demonstrates clear application of ATL skills to achieve your product.
You demonstrate robust research skills by evaluating medical sources for reliability, cross-checking evidence to minimize misinformation.
Your consultation with a chemistry teacher and designers provided valuable expert input, directly enriching your design elements and research depth.
You provide detailed examples of sewing techniques learned and challenges overcome, which underscores effective skill acquisition and enhances your productâs quality.
The teacher feedback form yields quantitative data but lacks qualitative insights. Include open-ended questions to capture detailed reasoning in future evaluations.
Your reflection on product evaluation is general. Structure your reflection to assess each success criterion explicitly with supporting evidence for a more thorough analysis.
You reflect thoughtfully on material technique adjustments (switching to fabric glue), demonstrating critical problem-solving and adaptability in enhancing your productâs precision.
Your reflection articulates clear skills gained (time management, problem-solving, sewing), effectively linking experiences to personal growth.
Teacher suggestions about creating a collection in varied colorways are promising. Strengthen your reflection by outlining how you would implement this idea in a future project.
